payloads.subject: ACTASAP fit check: Aureon as non-funded evidence validation collaborator
payloads.public_channel: QUB public Innovation Programmes contacts; ACTASAP IFS route.
payloads.body:
Dear QUB Innovation Programmes team,

I am Gary Leckey of R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd in Belfast. Aureon is coordinating our grant evidence workflow for the ACTASAP Pilot Phase 1 route, which closes on 28 July 2026 at 11:00.

The route appears to require a UK registered academic institution with an ACT-related idea, support from the institution's technology transfer office or equivalent, and a no-subsidy / no selective commercial advantage position. R&A is not presenting itself as the academic lead or funding claimant.

We are seeking a rapid fit check on whether an academic team at your institution could lead or advise on an ACTASAP concept where Aureon would act only as a non-funded evidence validation and market-discovery collaborator. The possible theme is advanced connectivity evidence automation: audit trails, autonomous workflow governance, and compliance records for connected systems and AI-assisted operator processes.

Before any IFS application can be completed, we need to evidence:

- an eligible academic lead with an ACT-related idea;
- TTO, commercialisation office or equivalent support;
- whether a non-funded industry validation role for R&A/Aureon is permitted;
- no-subsidy and no selective commercial advantage fit;
- project lead availability, programme dates and eligible cost basis.

Could you direct this to the right academic commercialisation contact, and advise whether a short fit call is possible before the deadline?

Regards,

Gary Leckey
R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd
Aureon grant operator workflow
gaxlec@gmail.com

payloads.subject: ACTASAP fit check: Aureon as non-funded evidence validation collaborator
payloads.public_channel: Ulster Innovation Services public Get in Touch route; no direct generic email selected.
payloads.body:
Target: Ulster University Innovation Services
Status: CONTACT_ROUTE_PAYLOAD_ONLY_NOT_SUBMITTED
Route: IFS ACTASAP Pilot Phase 1

Suggested contact message:

I am Gary Leckey of R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd in Belfast. Aureon is coordinating our grant evidence workflow for ACTASAP Pilot Phase 1, closing 28 July 2026 at 11:00.

The route appears to require a UK registered academic institution with an ACT-related idea, support from the institution's technology transfer office or equivalent, and a no-subsidy / no selective commercial advantage position. R&A is not presenting itself as the academic lead or funding claimant.

We are seeking a rapid fit check on whether an Ulster academic team could lead or advise on an ACTASAP concept where Aureon would act only as a non-funded evidence validation and market-discovery collaborator. The possible theme is advanced connectivity evidence automation: audit trails, autonomous workflow governance, and compliance records for connected systems and AI-assisted operator processes.

Evidence needed before any IFS application can be completed: eligible academic lead, TTO or equivalent support, non-funded collaborator permission, no-subsidy fit, project lead availability, programme dates and eligible cost basis.

Please direct this to the correct Innovation Services, commercialisation or academic lead contact.

Gary Leckey
R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd
gaxlec@gmail.com

payloads.subject: Full ADOPT fit check: farmer-led evidence automation trial route
payloads.public_channel: ADOPT Support Hub public email from IFS/Farming Innovation Programme/Business Connect pages.
payloads.body:
Dear ADOPT Support Hub,

I am Gary Leckey of R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd in Belfast. Aureon is coordinating our grant evidence workflow for the Full ADOPT Grant Round 8 route closing on 29 July 2026 at 11:00, and the ADOPT Facilitator route where relevant.

R&A is not presenting itself as an England farming, growing or forestry lead. We are seeking the correct route for an evidence automation work package that could support a farmer-led on-farm trial if an eligible farming lead and registered Project Facilitator are identified.

The possible project theme is farm evidence automation: structured field records, audit trails, workflow prompts, trial evidence capture and reporting support for practical on-farm AI or digital adoption experiments.

Before any IFS application can be completed, we need to evidence:

- an active farming, growing or forestry lead based in England;
- at least one other UK farmer, grower or forester claiming grant funding;
- a registered ADOPT Project Facilitator;
- on-farm problem statement and trial site;
- eligible GBP50,000 to GBP200,000 project budget and partner cost split;
- whether R&A/Aureon could be a collaborator, subcontract-style supplier where allowed, or non-funded support role.

Could you advise the correct Support Hub / facilitator / partner route and whether this evidence-automation role is potentially in scope for a farmer-led ADOPT project?

Regards,

Gary Leckey
R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd
Aureon grant operator workflow
gaxlec@gmail.com

payloads.target: Innovate UK Business Connect ADOPT facilitator route
payloads.subject: ADOPT facilitator route query: evidence automation SME for farmer-led trial
payloads.public_channel: ADOPT facilitator route listed by the Farming Innovation Programme.
payloads.body:
Dear Innovate UK Business Connect ADOPT facilitator team,

I am Gary Leckey of R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd in Belfast. Aureon is coordinating our grant evidence workflow for ADOPT routes, including Full ADOPT Grant Round 8 and the facilitator-supported application path.

R&A is not presenting itself as the farming lead. We are seeking guidance on how to connect an evidence automation work package with an eligible farmer-led project and a registered ADOPT Project Facilitator.

The possible contribution is practical trial evidence capture: field-record structure, audit trails, workflow prompts, reporting records and adoption evidence for an on-farm AI or digital workflow experiment.

Before any IFS application can be completed, we need to evidence:

- eligible England farming, growing or forestry lead;
- UK farmer/grower/forester collaborator;
- registered Project Facilitator;
- farm problem, trial site and trial method;
- budget owner and eligible cost split;
- the permitted role for R&A/Aureon.

Could you advise the correct facilitator registration or matching route, and whether an evidence automation SME can be introduced into a farmer-led ADOPT proposal?

Regards,

Gary Leckey
R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd
Aureon grant operator workflow
gaxlec@gmail.com

diligence_notes: R&A/Aureon is not represented as an academic lead for ACTASAP.
R&A/Aureon is not represented as an England farming, growing or forestry lead for ADOPT.
No academic lead, TTO support, farming lead, facilitator, trial site, partner budget or eligible-cost split has been received from these drafts.
Companies House confirmation-statement filing risk remains active in the internal ledger and is not included in outreach copy unless a recipient asks for due-diligence detail.

why_no_submission: ACTASAP still requires an eligible academic lead, ACT-related academic idea and TTO support.
Full ADOPT still requires an eligible England farming lead, UK farming collaborator, registered facilitator, trial site and budget.
Draft outreach payloads do not constitute partner commitment or route eligibility evidence.
External email sends, contact-route submissions and IFS portal edits remain action-time approval events.

controls: No IFS portal field was changed.
No partner invitation, upload, award terms acceptance or Project Impact action was performed.
No unsupported academic lead, farming lead, TTO, facilitator, trial site, finance or budget claim is made.
Payloads use formal recipient-facing language and exclude internal operating notes.
Action-time approval is required for each exact recipient and payload before any external send or contact-route submission.
